title: Glasgow Prestwick Airport e-commerce employment boost

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Glasgow Prestwick Airport said recent e-commerce growth has lifted employment, with 150 new jobs created and numbers forecast to rise to 200 by the end of the summer. Chief executive Ian Forgie said the increase in headcount was tied to expanding services and would benefit both the airport and the Ayrshire economy.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Forgie said the airport had spent recent months onboarding staff for the new services. He added that scheduled daily e-commerce flights between China and Prestwick would allow the airport to play a bigger role in Scottish salmon and whisky exports direct from Scotland to China and should lead to further employment opportunities.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Alan Gemmell, MP for Central Ayrshire, visited the airport to see its equipment and handling operation and said he would support its growth plans. He said Prestwick had new e-commerce contracts with two Chinese flag carriers, could process and offload cargo up to two days faster than any other UK airport, and now had 150 e-commerce staff.
